name: React
summary: Declarative component-based UI library maintained by Meta with the largest frontend ecosystem.

name: Vue
summary: Progressive framework with an approachable API, reactivity system, and single-file components.

name: Svelte
summary: Compile-time reactive framework that shifts work from runtime to build step for smaller bundles.

name: Solid
summary: Fine-grained reactive framework with JSX syntax and no virtual DOM for top-tier performance.

name: Angular
summary: Full-featured opinionated framework by Google with dependency injection, RxJS, and a complete CLI.

name: Preact
summary: 3kB React-compatible alternative with the same API for size-constrained applications.

name: Lit
summary: Google-backed library for building fast, lightweight web components with reactive templates.

name: Alpine.js
summary: Lightweight declarative framework for adding behavior directly in HTML markup.

name: Htmx
summary: HTML-over-the-wire library that adds AJAX, WebSockets, and SSE via attributes without JavaScript.

name: Qwik
summary: Resumability-first framework by Builder.io that eliminates hydration for instant interactivity.

name: Inferno
summary: Performance-focused React-like library optimized for rendering speed and small bundle size.

name: Mithril
summary: Tiny full-featured SPA framework with built-in routing and XHR in under 10kB.

name: Stencil
summary: Compiler by Ionic that generates framework-agnostic web components from TypeScript and JSX.

name: Marko
summary: eBay-developed streaming UI framework with automatic partial hydration and HTML-first templating.

name: Next.js
summary: Vercel's full-stack React framework with SSR, SSG, API routes, and App Router.

name: Nuxt
summary: Vue-based meta-framework with file-based routing, SSR, auto-imports, and Nitro server engine.

name: SvelteKit
summary: Official Svelte application framework with filesystem routing, SSR, and adapter-based deployment.

name: Astro
summary: Content-focused framework shipping zero JS by default with island architecture for partial hydration.

name: Remix
summary: Web-standards-focused React framework emphasizing progressive enhancement and nested routing.

name: SolidStart
summary: SolidJS meta-framework with file-based routing, SSR, and Nitro-powered server functions.

name: Gatsby
summary: GraphQL-powered React static site generator with a rich plugin ecosystem, now in maintenance mode.

name: Qwik City
summary: Qwik's meta-framework layer adding file-based routing, data loaders, and server-side actions.

name: Fresh
summary: Deno-native framework using Preact with island architecture and zero build step.

name: Analog
summary: Vite-powered Angular meta-framework with file-based routing and SSR via Nitro.

name: Redwood
summary: Opinionated full-stack React framework with GraphQL, Prisma ORM, and cell-based data fetching.

name: Vite
summary: Lightning-fast dev server and build tool using native ES modules and Rollup for production.

name: Webpack
summary: Mature and highly configurable module bundler that defined the modern JS build pipeline.

name: esbuild
summary: Go-based JavaScript bundler and minifier that is 10-100x faster than traditional tools.

name: Turbopack
summary: Rust-based incremental bundler by Vercel designed as the successor to Webpack for Next.js.

name: Rollup
summary: ES module bundler focused on tree-shaking and clean output, used internally by Vite.

name: Parcel
summary: Zero-configuration bundler with automatic transforms and built-in dev server.

name: Rspack
summary: Rust-based Webpack-compatible bundler by ByteDance offering drop-in migration with 5-10x speed.

name: SWC
summary: Rust-based JavaScript/TypeScript compiler used by Next.js and Vite as a Babel replacement.

name: Bun Bundler
summary: Built-in bundler for the Bun runtime with esbuild-compatible API and native speed.

name: tsup
summary: Zero-config TypeScript library bundler powered by esbuild with DTS generation.

name: unbuild
summary: Unified build system from the UnJS ecosystem using Rollup and esbuild with passive bundling.

name: Tailwind CSS
summary: Utility-first CSS framework that composes designs directly in markup with JIT compilation.

name: shadcn/ui
summary: Copy-paste component collection built on Radix UI and Tailwind CSS that you own and customize.

name: Radix UI
summary: Unstyled accessible React primitives for building high-quality design systems.

name: Chakra UI
summary: Accessible React component library with a composable style-props API and theming system.

name: MUI
summary: Material Design React components with extensive theming, a data grid, and date pickers.

name: Ant Design
summary: Enterprise-grade React UI library by Alibaba with 50+ polished components and design tokens.

name: Mantine
summary: Full-featured React component library with 100+ hooks, form handling, and rich text editor.

name: DaisyUI
summary: Framework-agnostic Tailwind CSS component library with semantic class names and 30+ themes.

name: UnoCSS
summary: Instant atomic CSS engine with Tailwind/Windi CSS preset compatibility and regex-based rules.

name: Panda CSS
summary: Type-safe CSS-in-JS engine with build-time extraction from the Chakra UI team.

name: vanilla-extract
summary: Zero-runtime CSS-in-TypeScript with static extraction and type-safe theme contracts.

name: Styled Components
summary: CSS-in-JS library using tagged template literals to style React components with scoped styles.

name: Emotion
summary: Performant CSS-in-JS library with both string and object styles, used as MUI's styling engine.

name: Headless UI
summary: Unstyled accessible UI components by Tailwind Labs for React and Vue.

name: Ark UI
summary: Headless component library from Chakra UI creators supporting React, Vue, and Solid via Zag.js.

name: Bootstrap
summary: The original responsive CSS framework with grid system, components, and JavaScript plugins.

name: Bulma
summary: CSS-only framework with Flexbox-based grid and clean class naming, no JavaScript required.

name: Zustand
summary: Minimal unopinionated React state management using hooks with no boilerplate or providers.

name: Jotai
summary: Primitive and flexible atomic state management for React inspired by Recoil.

name: Redux Toolkit
summary: Official opinionated Redux toolset with createSlice, RTK Query, and Immer-based reducers.

name: Pinia
summary: Official Vue state management with Composition API support, devtools, and SSR compatibility.

name: XState
summary: Framework-agnostic finite state machines and statecharts with a visual editor and actor model.

name: Valtio
summary: Proxy-based reactive state for React that makes mutable state automatically tracked.

name: Recoil
summary: Meta's experimental atomic state library for React with derived data and async queries.

name: MobX
summary: Transparent reactive state management using observables with minimal boilerplate.

name: Nanostores
summary: Tiny framework-agnostic state manager designed for multi-framework islands architecture.

name: Effector
summary: Reactive state manager with explicit data flow using stores, events, and effects.

name: TanStack Store
summary: Framework-agnostic reactive store powering TanStack Router and TanStack Form internals.

name: Ember.js
summary: Convention-over-configuration framework with strong backward compatibility and Glimmer rendering.

name: Eleventy
summary: Zero-config static site generator supporting 10+ template languages with no client JS by default.

name: Biome
summary: Rust-based all-in-one formatter and linter replacing Prettier and ESLint with a single tool.

name: Lightning CSS
summary: Rust-based CSS parser, transformer, and minifier replacing PostCSS and Autoprefixer.

name: PrimeVue
summary: Rich Vue component suite with 90+ components, themes, and optional Tailwind integration.

name: Vuetify
summary: Material Design component framework for Vue with extensive theming and enterprise features.

name: Skeleton
summary: Tailwind-based adaptive UI toolkit originally for Svelte, now expanding to React.

name: Open Props
summary: CSS custom properties library providing consistent design tokens without a build step.

name: Signals (TC39)
summary: TC39 Stage 1 proposal for native JavaScript reactivity primitives with polyfill implementations.

name: TanStack Query
summary: Framework-agnostic async state manager for server data with caching, refetching, and mutations.

name: Observable Framework
summary: Observable Framework is a static site generator for data-driven pages, with reactive JavaScript, SQL code blocks, and built-in DuckDB-WASM support.
